assumptions.role: Defines the static action and stationary equation under test.
assumptions.statement: The imported Bianconi metric action is evaluated on a real non-diagonal SPD block.

assumptions.role: Prevents a local-attractor result from being misstated globally.
assumptions.statement: The stable branch g > sigma is the local basin of interest; the action is not claimed globally bounded near the SPD boundary.
Frobenius and affine-invariant SPD metrics are both legitimate but non-unique dissipative completion choices.

assumptions.role: Exposes the dependence of the exponent on correlations.
assumptions.statement: The fluctuation observable is a centered finite-variance sum with the explicitly named stationary covariance kernel.

assumptions.role: Marks the Sorkin-style observable identification, not a specimen result.
assumptions.statement: Lambda_eff is identified with RMS fluctuation per cell after the raw extensive mean is removed.

assumptions.role: Limits the result to disclosure dynamics, not cell issuance.
assumptions.statement: The tested substrate has fixed cell count N.

comparators: iid versus short-range, long-range, and global covariance
raw extensive mean versus centered fluctuation density

concept.invariant: Lambda reads a dynamically sourced deviation of effective influence from uniform, is baseline at uniform, and grows monotonically with concentration.

equations: S(G)=sigma log det G + Tr[G(log G-log G_ind)]-Tr G
dot G_AI=-G(grad_E S)G under the affine-invariant SPD metric
falsifiers: A dissipative completion misses the shared stationary equation in its stable basin.
The two gradient vector fields are collinear on the generic noncommuting fixture.
Long-range or global covariance retains the iid half-power exponent.
The raw stationary vacuum term becomes subextensive without an added constraint.

free_choices.choice: sigma=0.2 and one generic 3x3 noncommuting SPD fixture
free_choices.sensitivity_test: The claims are expressed as structural checks: noncommutation, stable-branch convergence, and covariance exponents, not fitted values.
free_choices.why_not_forced: The specimen does not select this coupling or fixture.

free_choices.sensitivity_test: Compare vector-field collinearity, action descent, and endpoint agreement; include an isospectral conservative no-relaxation control.
free_choices.why_not_forced: A variational action fixes extrema, not a mobility metric.

free_choices.sensitivity_test: Use asymptotic classes: summable covariance should retain -1/2; power-law alpha should give -alpha/2; global covariance should give 0.
free_choices.why_not_forced: Neither correlation kernel is supplied by the action.

free_choices.choice: Block replication as the mean-screening discriminator
free_choices.sensitivity_test: A real screen must make the raw term subextensive; constant density under exact replication is an explicit no-screening result.
free_choices.why_not_forced: No cosmological coupling or renormalization rule is built.

null_models: Isospectral orthogonal Lax flow: SPD dynamics without relaxation
Global common mode: an extensive sum without CLT suppression
observables: initial vector-field noncollinearity and endpoint agreement
tail log-log slope of fluctuation density in four covariance classes
raw vacuum-term growth and density under block replication
candidate_contract.question: Which stationary, trajectory, fluctuation-scaling, and mean-screening claims survive legitimate completions of the Bianconi SPD entropy action?

checks.name: matrix gradient matches a noncommuting finite-difference direction
specimen is genuinely non-diagonal and noncommuting
Euclidean completion dissipates S and reaches the stable equation
affine-invariant completion dissipates S and reaches the stable equation
legitimate dissipative completions have non-collinear vector fields
dissipative completions agree on the stationary metric
conservative isospectral control preserves S and does not relax
iid fluctuations give the half-power density
summable short-range correlations retain the half-power asymptote
non-summable long-range correlations change the exponent
one global mode gives an unsuppressed density
extensivity alone does not determine the fluctuation exponent
NO-SCREENING control: raw vacuum mean stays extensive

result.claim: The two tested dissipative completions share the stable stationary metric but not the trajectory; the half-power survives iid and summable mixing correlations, fails for long-range/global correlation, and the raw extensive mean is not screened.
result.grade: PARTIAL — completion-robust local fixed point and mixing-class half-power; completion not selected, observable imported, mean unscreened.
result.remaining_uncertainty: Only one finite non-diagonal block and two dissipative mobilities are tested; covariance calculations use linearized centered fluctuations; no cell growth, cosmological response, or native subtraction mechanism exists.

stop_conditions: Stop claiming completion-robust dynamics if modest legitimate SPD mobilities change the stationary observable rather than only its path.
Stop saying the half-power is forced by extensivity alone; retain it only for covariance classes with Var(sum)=Theta(N).
Stop the Bianconi mean-screening route unless a separately specified constraint or dynamics makes the raw mean subextensive without hand subtraction.
Any failure here is local to the Bianconi completion formalization; CONCEPT-DU-001 remains open absent an invariant-level no-go.

comparison_receipt.note: Contract completeness means legible and comparable, not physically true. Warrant types are non-ordinal; computation or formal argument disposes.

parameters.flow_tolerance_note: Float64 Armijo action differences reach their precision floor at residual O(1e-8); endpoint agreement is checked independently.

scope.concept: A failure is local to this adjacent formalization and does not close CONCEPT-DU-001.
scope.fixed_point: Robust across the two tested dissipative metric choices in one local stable basin.
scope.half_power: Conditional on Var(sum)=Theta(N), satisfied by iid and summable mixing covariance.
scope.mean: Not screened by the tested action or completions.
scope.trajectory: Not robust: the initial vector fields are non-collinear.
